g.data-narration: Welcome to Raspberry Pi for IoT! Today we'll explore how this tiny computer connects the physical world to the digital cloud.
The Raspberry Pi is a small, affordable single-board computer developed in the UK by the Raspberry Pi Foundation for education.
g.text: Small, affordable single-board computer developed in the UK by
the Raspberry Pi Foundation for teaching computer science.

g.data-narration: An IoT device transmits data over a network without human interaction. The Pi can sense, process, and communicate like no other!
g.text: What Makes it an IoT Device?
IoT device = Hardware that transmits data over a network without

g.data-narration: Three popular Pi models serve different IoT needs: Zero W for ultra-low power, Pi 3B+ as a gateway, and Pi 4 for high-performance edge computing.
The 40-pin GPIO header is the most important IoT feature. It connects sensors, actuators, LEDs, and electronic components directly to the Pi.
g.text: General Purpose Input/Output: Programmable pins to read sensors or control actuators.

g.data-narration: Three key protocols connect devices over GPIO: I2C for multi-device sensors, SPI for high-speed communication, and UART for point-to-point serial.
I2C uses just two wires to connect multiple devices. The Pi acts as master, communicating with slave devices like sensors through SDA and SCL lines.
Raspberry Pi OS (Debian-based) runs on the Pi. Key concepts include the kernel, shell, packages, daemons, and permissions for security.
Python is the go-to language for Pi IoT. RPi.GPIO handles low-level pin control, while gpiozero provides beginner-friendly component abstractions.
g.text: Python Libraries for Raspberry Pi IoT
Low-level pin control: setmode, setup, output, cleanup

g.data-narration: This example shows RPi.GPIO controlling an LED. We set BCM mode, configure pin 17 as output, then toggle it HIGH and LOW in a loop.
A weather station uses BME280 sensor over I2C to measure temperature, humidity, and pressure. The Pi collects and sends data to the cloud.
The Python code reads BME280 data via I2C using smbus2, then POSTs the readings as JSON to a cloud API every 5 minutes.
Raspberry Pi uses two numbering systems: BCM (Broadcom chip numbering) and Physical (pin position on header). GPIO.setmode() selects between them.
Beyond GPIO, the Pi offers USB ports for peripherals, CSI for camera modules, and DSI for touchscreen displays - expanding IoT capabilities.
For exams: memorize Pi model specs, know GPIO protocols, practice reading Python GPIO code, and understand basic Linux commands for the Pi.
g.text: 1. Pi Models: Zero W (~0.5W, 512MB) vs 3B+ (~3-4W, 1GB) vs 4B (~3-7W, up to 8GB)
2. Protocols: I²C (SDA+SCL, multi-device), SPI (MOSI/MISO/SCLK, high-speed), UART (Tx/Rx, point-to-point)
3. Python: RPi.GPIO vs gpiozero, BCM numbering, basic LED/button code reading

g.data-narration: Raspberry Pi combines Linux OS, GPIO for hardware, and networking - making it perfect for IoT. Build sensor nodes, gateways, or edge computing devices!
g.text: 🎯 Raspberry Pi: Your Gateway to IoT Innovation!
